what_it_is: This repository appears to be a Next.js parallax story composition platform with a layered backend, Prisma-backed persistence, and feature-oriented UI plus API workflows for projects, scenes, uploads, preview, and authentication.

plain_english.purpose: It looks like a web app for composing parallax-style story projects, managing scenes and uploads, and previewing playback results.
plain_english.how_it_is_organized: The code is split into app routes, feature UI, HTTP controllers, domain modules, core utilities, infrastructure integrations, and Prisma data definitions.
plain_english.how_requests_move: Browser or API requests enter through Next.js routes, pass into controllers, then into module services and repositories or infrastructure adapters.
plain_english.where_the_main_logic_lives: Most business logic appears to live in `src/modules/**`, while HTTP-specific orchestration sits in `src/interfaces/http/**` and UI composition sits in `src/features/**`.

patterns: Request handling appears to flow from Next.js route files into controller files and then into module services, matching a layered application design.
UI concerns are separated into `src/features/**`, while reusable business logic lives in `src/modules/**`, reducing coupling between presentation and domain logic.
Persistence and provider integrations are isolated under infrastructure and Prisma layers, suggesting deliberate boundaries around external systems.
The versioned API surface under `src/app/api/v1/**` indicates an intentional external contract rather than ad hoc route growth.

critical_flows.name: Authentication flow
critical_flows.description: Auth requests enter through NextAuth routes, rely on auth setup, and likely resolve users through the auth repository and Prisma client.

critical_flows.name: Project management flow
critical_flows.description: Project CRUD and orchestration likely move from API route to controller, through project service logic, into repository-backed persistence.

critical_flows.name: Scene processing flow
critical_flows.description: Scene regeneration appears to combine scene orchestration, processing logic, and Qwen provider integration.

critical_flows.name: Preview playback flow
critical_flows.description: The preview UI likely requests playback data from the API, which is then composed by preview and playback services.
